title: Estepona Catamaran Rental Group Guide: Costs, Tips & Boats in 2026
html:
Planning a group getaway in Estepona? Hiring a catamaran from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona offers a spacious and social way to explore the Costa del Sol, with prices typically from EUR 1,200 for a full-day charter on a 40-foot vessel. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a seamless booking experience.

Where to set off in Estepona

Most catamaran charters depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, the main marina with modern facilities and easy access to the open sea. Nearby Puerto de la Duquesa (10 minutes east) and Sotogrande (20 minutes northeast) offer alternative launch points if you are staying further along the coast. Popular cruising grounds include Bahía de Estepona, the calm waters off Punta Doncella, and the islands of Isla de las Palomas and Isla de Tarifa (about 40 nautical miles south). Many skippers also sail to Marbella for a lunch stop, just 8 nautical miles east.

Which boat to rent

  1. Catamaran (40, 50 ft) Best for groups of 8, 12 people seeking stability and deck space; from EUR 1,200 for a full day.
  2. Motor yacht (Azimut 50, 60 ft) Ideal for luxury cruising with up to 10 guests; from EUR 2,500 per day.
  3. Sailing yacht (Lagoon 42 ft) Perfect for a more traditional experience with 6, 8 guests; from EUR 900 half-day.
  4. RIB or open speedboat (up to 30 ft) Great for small groups of 4, 6 who want to waterski or explore coves; from EUR 400 half-day.

For the Shibuli, a popular 40-foot Leopard catamaran available through BoatHire24, visit the Shibuli listing for full specs and availability.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona?

Prices vary by boat type and season, with catamaran group charters starting from EUR 1,200 for a full day. Here is a typical cost breakdown:

  • Shared group tour (1, 2 hours): from EUR 50 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): from EUR 300
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from EUR 1,500
  • Crewed luxury yacht (up to 10 guests with skipper and host): from EUR 2,000

To compare prices and find the best option for your group, use BoatHire24 to browse the full range of yacht charter Estepona offers.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona?

For bareboat catamaran rentals, a Spanish recreational boating licence is typically required, but most groups opt for a skippered charter which requires no licence. The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. The high season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can freshen afternoon breezes.

In Estepona, catamaran charters often include a swim stop at the serene Bahía de Estepona, where the water is calmer and clearer than the main Costa del Sol beaches.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,500 per day for group catamarans
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 4-hour half-day or 8-hour full-day
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests (plus crew)
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Estepona

Can I book a catamaran for a large group in Estepona?

Yes, many catamarans in Estepona accommodate up to 12 passengers, with options to add extra crew or catering for celebrations.

What is the best time of year to rent a catamaran in Estepona?

The summer months from June to September offer the warmest weather and calmest seas, though spring and early autumn also provide excellent conditions with fewer crowds.

Are there any hidden fees for catamaran rental in Estepona?

Always confirm whether fuel, skipper gratuity, and marina fees are included; many charters quote a flat rate including these, but it is wise to ask the operator directly.

Can I bring my own food and drink on a catamaran charter?

Most skippered charters allow you to bring your own provisions, though some operators offer catering packages as an optional extra.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
